observations.recommendation:
3. The Municipal Accountant undertakes a thorough review and analysis of the details of the accounts payable and effects the necessary adjustments to revert long outstanding and invalid claims to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund.  

4. The Municipal Accountant refrains from recognizing obligations and recording of Accounts Payable for those claims not properly and completely documented to present fairly the expenses and payable accounts in the financial statements of the LGU.

7. Management requires the concerned officials to submit a written justification on the above-mentioned observation and henceforth refrain from incurring and charging expenses to the LDRRMF those that are not related to disaster risk reduction and management activities.

13. Municipal Treasurer conducts intensive tax campaign, employ collection strategies and institute legal remedies pursuant to the pertinent provisions of RA 7160 to persuade taxpayers to pay their real property tax dues on time.

14. Management revises/ updates its Revenue Code pursuant to Section 191 of R.A.7160 to increase the revenue collection of the municipality to provide additional funds which can be used to finance beneficial programs and projects.

15.	Management requires the Municipal Accountant to maintain RPLC prescribed in the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Volume 2, update the land account as per subsidiary ledger and reconcile with the record of the Office of the Municipal Assessor.

16. 	Management retrieves or secures the documentary requirements as required in COA Circular 2012-001 pertaining to all lot acquisitions and submits the same to this Office.

17. Management creates a committee to work on the titling of all LGU-owned lots, including among others, appropriation of funds for the purpose.

18. Management keeps and maintains an updated file of the real properties acquired by the LGU.  Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a Real Property Ledger Card to record the acquisition, description, addition/improvements, disposal and other information for each class of real property (land). Transactions affecting each real property such as cost of survey, etc. shall be posted from source documents promptly therein.
19. Management revisits COA Circular No. 2020-006 and ensure full implementation of “One time cleansing of PPE accounts” in coordination with the offices concerned.

20.Management implements immediately and accordingly the purpose of trust receipts. Otherwise, evaluate the need of these unexpended balances of aid/financial assistance from different source agencies. 

21. Management returns immediately any unutilized balances to the source agency should these funds prove to be no longer needed.

22. Management conducts actual studies for previously identified projects and determine if those are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, consider for their reversion to release the funds and make available to some meaningful use.

23. Management requires the preparation and submission of the Monthly Report of Official Travels for every vehicle and the Report of Fuel Consumption for evaluation.  


24. Management submits a copy of the specific internal control measures installed in connection with the disbursements for fuel, oil and lubricants expenses.

25. The Municipal Budget Officer evaluates and reviews the budget proposals well before it is submitted to the Local Sanggunian for enactment.  It is necessary that the budget be thoroughly reviewed to avoid incidence of bloated income estimates which can affect sound financial management of the agency.

27. We recommend that management require the officials/employees concerned to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances. Withhold any claim/payment of these personnel concerned especially those who already retired from the service until such time their cash advances are fully liquidated.

26. The Inventory Committee regularly conducts the annual physical inventory of PPEs.

27.Management undertakes the periodic reconciliation of the Accounting and Supply Unit’s records.

28. Management requires the LDRRMO to submit the LDRRMFIP at the start of the year and the Monthly Report of the Utilization of LDRRMF duly certified by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to recognize the restoration of the cash account and the appropriate liability/payable account by ₱2,741,730.44.  We further recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the RCI and include only the checks already released to claimants and forward it to the Municipal Accountant for recording.

We recommended and management agreed that:
the Inventory Committee prepare a Report of Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN) using the prescribed format per COA Circular No. 2015-008.
the Municipal Accountant maintain a subsidiary record for roads and road components for every road network
The Municipal Accountant prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
(d)  the GSO maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all the roads and their components.
(e) the Municipal Accountant include the required disclosures in the Notes to FS.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
(a)  Revisit its revenue forecasting methods to achieve a realistic projection of income.
(b)  Require the Municipal Budget Officer to evaluate and review the budget proposals well before it is submitted to the Local Sanggunian for enactment.
(c)  Establish a system of financial monitoring to ensure adherence to the spending limit of the DMI.

We recommended that the personnel concerned submit written justification on the incurrence of the above-mentioned transactions. Further, it is recommended that the municipality immediately stop the practice of providing fuel and gasoline to privately-owned vehicles.
We recommend that management provide a written justification for not imposing liquidated damages on the subject transaction.

We recommended that management submit an explanation of their non-adherence to the provisions of RA No. 10752 on the acquisition of lot by government agencies. We further recommended that management ensure the transfer of ownership is completed by allocating the necessary funds to cover the payment of capital gains tax, Documentary Stamp Tax and other transfer fees.

We recommended that management:
a. Require from the concerned personnel who was responsible in the missed PhilHealth contribution the refund of the amount of ₽36,665.44.
b. Assign a personnel who would be responsible in monitoring and reviewing of the remittances to PhilHealth and other government agencies’ contribution to avoid the recurrence of missed remittance.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer immediately implement the proper maintenance of the check register by the assigned personnel using a logbook, adhering to the prescribed format and including all the necessary details, in order to ensure that relevant data as to check issuance are safeguarded from loss.

We recommended and Management agreed to comply with the requirements of Section 1 of EO No. 662 by facilitating the creation and maintenance of an active official LGU website and require the BAC to assign specific BAC personnel to take charge of the timely posting of the required reports in the website.

1. The Municipal Accountant computes and records the accumulated depreciation for the above-mentioned land improvements and consider the following adjusting journal entries:

	Prior Year’s Adjustment	Xxx
			Accumulated Depreciation	Xxx	

2. The Municipal Accountant undertakes the reclassification of the assets mentioned so that the accurate balances thereof are reported in the financial statements.

5. The Municipal Accountant (MA) prepares and effects the necessary adjustments on the unrecorded transactions/ reconciling items identified in the Bank Reconciliation Statements.

6. The MA communicates officially to the concerned official or employee to ensure immediate action in cases wherein adjustments require supporting documents or action by other person/s.

8. Management submits an explanation/ justification on the utilization of the 20% LDF for purposes that are considered deviations from Section 3.2.5 of the JMC. 

9. Management refrains from charging against the 20% LDF those expenditures that are intended for administrative purposes so that optimal utilization thereof may be achieved.

10. Management requires the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the CY 2022 and 2023 balances of the unexpended/ unobligated Quick Response Fund and 70% Mitigation Fund-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund account to ensure that funds are utilized only for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction management as required by laws, rules and regulations.

11. Management designates another qualified Special Disbursing Officer if additional cash advances are necessary and justified under the circumstances; or

12. Management increases the amount of fidelity bond of the Disbursing Officer to sufficiently cover cash accountabilities.

29. The Municipal Treasurer safeguards the accountable forms by always keeping it in a secured place preferably in a locked fireproof or safe in a locked office strictly accessible to authorized personnel only.

We recommended and Management agreed to: 

3)	Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain RPLC prescribed in the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Volume 2,  update the land account as per subsidiary ledger and reconcile with the record of the Office of the Municipal Assessor;

4)	Retrieve or secure the documentary requirements as required in COA Circular 2012-001 pertaining to all lot acquisitions and submit the same to this Office.

5)	Create a committee to work on the titling of all LGU-owned lots, including among others, appropriation of fund for the purpose.

6)  Keep and maintain an updated file of the real properties acquired by the LGU.  Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a Real Property Ledger Card to record the acquisition, description, addition/improvements, disposal and other information for each class of real property (land). Transactions affecting each real property such as cost of survey, etc. shall be posted from source documents promptly therein.
7. We recommended and management agreed to revisit COA Circular No. 2020-006 and ensure full implementation of “One time cleansing of PPE accounts” in coordination with the offices concerned.

8. We recommended that Management implement immediately and accordingly the purpose of trust receipts. Otherwise, evaluate the need of these unexpended balances of aid/financial assistance from different source agencies. 

9. Should these funds prove to be no longer needed, return immediately any unutilized balances to the source agency.

10. We recommended that management with the assistance of the MPDC and other key players in the decision making of the LGU strategize so that projects identified have to be implemented within the year to address the needs of the community and to get the maximum value of fund allocated for a specific period considering the increasing prices of materials.  

11. Further, conduct actual studies for previously identified projects and determine if those are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, consider for their reversion to release the funds and make available to some meaningful use.

12. We recommended and management agreed to cause the preparation and submission of the Monthly Report of Official Travels for every vehicle and the Report of Fuel Consumption for evaluation.  

13. We further recommended that management submit a copy of the specific internal control measures installed in connection with the disbursements for fuel, oil and lubricants expenses.

16. We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer evaluate and review the budget proposals well before it is submitted to the Local Sanggunian for enactment.  It is necessary that the budget be thoroughly reviewed to avoid incidence of bloated income estimates which can affect sound financial management of the agency.

18. We recommend that annual physical inventory of PPEs be regularly conducted by the Inventory Committee. 

19. Likewise, periodic reconciliation of Accounting and Supply Unit’s’ record be undertaken.

22. We recommended that Management require the LDRRMO to submit the LDRRMFIP at the start of the year and the Monthly Report of the Utilization of LDRRMF duly certified cored by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

23. We recommended that management require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to safeguard the accountable forms by keeping it in a securer place at all times preferably in a locked fireproof or safe in a locked office strictly accessible to authorized personnel only.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant compute and record the accumulated depreciation for the above-mentioned land improvements and consider the following adjusting journal entries:

Misclassification of various PPE accounts totaling ₽3,234,523.00
1.10 A review of the PPE Schedule showed various misclassified assets with regard to the account charged, as follows.
1.11 In an inquiry with the Municipal Accountant on the circumstance of the above observation, he stated that he has yet to review his report on file to determine the cause of the cited deficiency.
1.12 Meanwhile, a reference to Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December    1, 2015 showed the description of the affected accounts as follows:
1.13 Although the above misclassification did not affect the total reported PPE at year-end, the individual PPE accounts were not accurate since these were not reflective of its total cost and accumulated depreciation.
1.14 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant undertake the reclassification of the assets mentioned so that the accurate balances thereof are reported in the financial statements.

2.8 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant undertake a thorough review and analysis of the details of the accounts payable and effect the necessary adjustments to revert long outstanding and invalid claims to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund.
2.9 We further recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant refrain from recognizing obligations and recording of Accounts Payable for those claims not properly and completely documented to present fairly the expenses and payable accounts in the financial statements of the LGU.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant (MA) to prepare and effect the necessary adjustments on the unrecorded transactions/ reconciling items identified in the Bank Reconciliation Statements. In cases wherein adjustments require supporting documents or action by other person/s, we recommended that the MA communicate officially to the concerned official or employee to ensure immediate action.

5.8 We recommended that Management:
5.8.1. Submit an explanation/ justification on the utilization of the 20% LDF for purposes that are considered deviations from Section 3.2.5 of the JMC.

24. Require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Property Disposal Committee the application for disposal of properties which are unserviceable and no longer needed by the LGU in compliance with the above-cited provision.

We recommend management to: 

a. Require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Property Disposal Committee the application for disposal of properties which are unserviceable and no longer needed by the LGU in compliance with the above-cited provision.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer & GSO had already gathered necessary document to facilitate the disposal of the properties and closing this amount of the books shall be made after its final disposal.
observations.reason: Unserviceable assets are still carried in the books.

observations.recommendation:
a. Require the concerned local officials to observe austerity measures in the disbursement of funds and refrain from incurring unnecessary expenses to spare government funds from wastage.

b. Send only representatives to seminars and trainings those who are directly involved in the implementation of programs and projects whose jobs are related thereof.
observations.managementAction: An appeal for reconsideration on the ND had been made by the concerned officials.
observations.reason: No refund was effected to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management refrain from allowing any employee to undertake the collection activities of the local government unit if he/she is not a regular appointee and officially designated to perform collection function. Moreover, require all accountable officers to apply for the proper amount of fidelity bond sufficient to cover the handled cash accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: Close daily monitoring by the revenue collection officer was conducted to ensure all collections are remitted to treasury on a daily basis.
observations.reason: Unbonded casual employees are allowed to do collecting functions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

a. Require the Local Finance Committee to review the collection efficiency of the LGU,

b. Exercise taxing power as authorized under Section 142 to 150 of RA 7160

c. Expose other income generating activities that could pursue to increase its revenue.
observations.managementAction: The agency is currently updating the records of real tax payers and intensified its collection effort. A review also to the revenue code is currently made to identify earning potential and to update the rates.
observations.reason: Dependency on IRA

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:

a. Require the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly check and support all the necessary documents in the voucher before payment shall be effected to prove the validity, legality and propriety of all transactions.

Submit immediately the required supporting documents to prove the validity and accuracy of the concerned claims.
observations.managementAction: All signatures and documentary requirements are already complied.
observations.reason: Compliance on the deficiencies noted in the particular transactions was noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the department head of the health services sectors to include a certification on the Purchase Requests for all products and medicines.
observations.managementAction: Requisition of Drugs and Medicines - The agency had already secured certifications from the concerned health officer of the subject procurements.
observations.reason: Acted upon by management.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to fully release the shares intended to the Provincial Government and 35 barangays concerned pursuant to Section 271 of RA 7160 in order for these LGUs to utilize said fund for their operations.
observations.managementAction: The Treasurer's office had already updated its release of shares to the concerned agencies and remaining balance is already current.
observations.reason: Provincial and Barangay shares were released to the concerned LGUs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:
a. Maintain special account in the General Fund for the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities. Separate financial statements have to be prepared.

b. Prepare a monthly report on fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs using the prescribed format. 

c. Post the accumulated reports on the LGU’s website and in at least three (3) conspicuous places in the locality.

d. The Local Chief Executive (LCE) of the implementing city/municipality shall send a written notice when said reports have been posted on its website to the DBM, House Representatives, Senate of the Philippines, House committee on Appropriations, Senate Committee on Finance, and other offices where submission of reports is require.
observations.managementAction: The (SAGF) Special Account in the Gen Fund (BGCM) Bayanihan Grant Cities & Municipalities.  The amount was downloaded by the NGA to General Fund and a Subsidiary was maintained in a separate (RAO) Registry of Allotment and Obligations in the same fund with its total disbursement/utilization are fully accounted and reported.
observations.reason: Compliance on the recommendation stated in item (a) was noted.

observations.managementAction: BIR Penalties - The agency had forward to COA the flow of transactions for the remittance of tax to BIR and job description that may provide information as to who is responsible for the late remittance to the BIR with the hope that COA will advise to the agency as to person responsible based on the documents submitted.
observations.reason: The amount paid to BIR for tax penalties and surcharge is booked as receivables.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant review the schedule of outstanding checks and identify those which have become long outstanding/ stale. Require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the accountant with the original DV and its supporting documents for the preparation of JEV following the procedures prescribed to ensure recording of such cancellation.
observations.managementAction: Stale Checks - The Treasurer's office is currently replacing the new checks to various stale checks and still in the process of reviewing some stale checks issued in the prior years.
observations.reason: Outstanding checks schedule includes some checks which have become stale as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the officials/employees concerned to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances. Withhold any claim/payment of these personnel concerned especially those who already retired from the service until such time their cash advances are fully liquidated.
observations.managementAction: Monitoring of Liquidation of Cash Advance - the agency has issued a demand letter to advances not settled after the regulatory period the CAs should be liquidated but unfortunately some of the concerned did not liquidated their CA’s despite of the demand. The agency does not allow CAs to employees & officials with unliquidated balance.
observations.reason: The schedule of cash advances shows deficiencies in the grant and liquidation thereof. This is reiterated in Part II of this year’s AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that annual physical inventory of PPEs be regularly conducted by the Inventory Committee. Likewise, periodic reconciliation of Accounting and Supply Unit’s’ record be undertaken.
observations.managementAction: The agency is presently updating its record of PPE and will be forming a committee to work for the inventory this year 2021.
observations.reason: Annual physical inventory of PPEs was not done in 2021 since management prioritized/ focused on the disaster response (after the typhoon).

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management: 

a. Require the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System to coordinate and conducting a meeting with the different LGU offices and national government agencies involved in the implementation of the planned GAD programs and activities.

b. Require the concerned officials involved to provide justification on inn-implementation of GAD programs and activities.
observations.managementAction: The pandemic had hindered vital activities in the implementation of GAD thus minimal utilization of fund.  The DSWD now embracing the new normal procedures to conduct its activities had already maximize utilization of the subject fund.
observations.reason: Partial and unimplemented projects and activities under the GAD appropriations were noted at year-end of CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to submit the LDRRMFIP at the start of the year and the Monthly Report of the Utilization of LDRRMF duly certified cored by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO, Accountant, & Budget Officer are working together to comply with the submission of the CY 2021 report.
observations.reason: The Monthly Report on the Utilization of the LDRRMF is still due for submission to this Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management advice the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books to transfer the unexpended appropriation of the CY 2019 LDRRMF to a special trust fund under the Trust Fund intended solely for the purpose of supporting disaster reduction and management activities within the next five (5) years.
observations.managementAction: The accountant shall affect   adjustment this year end closing entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to safeguard the accountable forms by keeping it in a securer place at all times preferably in a locked fireproof or safe in a locked office strictly accessible to authorized personnel only.
observations.managementAction: Tracings is still conducted with some forms already retrieved and all accountable forms are already placed in a safe vault with only the Treasurer have access and logbook is properly maintained as to release of accountable forms.
observations.reason: A number of accountable forms were retrieved. However, two booklets remained unaccounted to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the official/s responsible of the lapses, in delaying the remittance of taxes, to refund the amount paid to BIR for penalties, interest, and surcharges.  Further, instruct the designated personnel to keep abreast with the BIR guidelines and instructions to avoid the occurrence of similar circumstance in the future.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the cancellation/adjustment of the stale checks in the reconciling items giving priority to long outstanding prior years’ issuances in order to reflect the correct balance of the asset account and the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommend that management require officials/employees concerned to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances. Withhold any claim/payment of these personnel concerned especially those who already retired from the service until such time their cash advances are fully liquidated.
Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant to monitor regularly the status of the cash advances for prompt reporting of the expenses incurred and safeguard as well as discourage the misuse of government funds on purposes other than what they are intended for.

We recommend that management:
Require the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System to coordinate and conduct meeting with the different LGU offices and national government agencies involved in the implementation of the planned GAD programs and activities.
Require the concerned officials involved to provide justification on non-implementation of GAD programs and activities.
Make an evaluation as to the clear-cut causes of the non-implementation of these projects and formulate strategies in order to ensure the complete implementation of GAD programs and activities in the future.

We recommend that annual physical inventory of PPEs be regularly conducted by the Inventory Committee. Likewise, periodic reconciliation of Accounting and the Property and Supply Units’ record be undertaken. Disposal of all unserviceable properties and those beyond economic repair should also be made to achieve fair presentation of the balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) at the start of the year and the Monthly Report on the Utilization of LDRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommend that management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly check and support all the necessary documents in the voucher before payment shall be effected to prove the validity, legality and propriety of the transactions.
Submit immediately the required supporting documents to prove the validity and accuracy of the concerned claims.
Further, all necessary data in the vouchers and supporting documents should be properly filled up and signed by authorized officials.

We recommend that management require the department head of the health services sectors to include a certification on the Purchase Requests for all drugs and medicines needs in accordance with E.O 49.  Henceforth, the BAC has to ensure that certifications are included in all PRs submitted for procurement of drugs and medicines, otherwise future similar deficiencies noted will be disallowed in audit.

Require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to monitor and control disbursements of the fund by not exceeding fifty (50%) of the uncollected estimated revenue in addition to the actual collections, provided, however, that no cash overdraft in any local fund shall be incurred at the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: Acted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management maximize the utilization of 70% of the LDRRMF appropriation to support disaster risk management activities and develop the resiliency of constituents in times of calamities.
observations.managementAction: Acted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to take action on our recommendation by requiring the Accountable Officer to restitute the amount due from him and/or institute administrative sanctions to recover the misappropriated funds.
observations.managementAction: Demanded the balance of unremitted amount from the previous  Municipal Treasurer

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to fully release the shares intended to the Provincial Government and 35 barangays concerned pursuant to Section 271 of RA 7160 in order for these LGUs to utilize said fund for their operations.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the Municipal Treasurer-ICO to fully release the amount due to the barangays

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant exert extra effort to review the long outstanding/stale checks specifically those checks without the identified payee. Require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the accountant with the original Disbursement Vouchers (DV) and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher following the procedures prescribed to ensure recording of such cancellation.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the Municipal Accountant to review long outstanding checks.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management advice the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books to transfer the unexpended appropriation of the CY 2019 LDRRMF to a special trust fund under the Trust Fund intended solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities within the next five (5) years.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of the quick response fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management  require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to safeguard the accountable forms by keeping it in a secure place at all times preferably in a locked fireproof or safe in a locked office strictly accessible to authorized personnel only to preclude the accountable forms from possible misuse and loss due to theft. Further, the logbook of accountable forms has to be monitored frequently.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the Municipal Treasurer-ICO to investigate the missing ORs

observations.recommendation:
We recommend management to:

Require the OIC- Municipal Treasurer and the Property Disposal Committee the application for disposal of properties which are unserviceable and no longer needed by the LGU in compliance with above- cited provision.

Drop from the books the value of the disposed properties after adjustments and approval.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the Municipal Accountant to dispose the unserviceable properties and drop them from the books

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to record all the transactions pertaining to collections and deposits of the General Fund daily or regularly pursuant to Sections 26 and 27 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs and in consonance with sound internal control in order to keep his official cash records intact and thus preclude the loss or pilferage thereof.
observations.managementAction: Acted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management exercise due diligence in the utilization and safekeeping of government properties. Moreover, require justification on the discrepancies in the quantity per purchase order against the actual physical count.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the Municipal Engineer to reprogram the  unused PVC pipes

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management totally relieve the Municipal Treasurer from his position and henceforth, preclude him from any designation/function that involves handling of cash. Further, require him to fully restitute the missing funds of ₱ 604,351.07.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the Municipal Treasurer-ICO to deposit all collections regularly

observations.recommendation:
Require concerned local officials to observe austerity measures in the disbursement of funds and refrain from incurring unnecessary expenses to spare government funds from wastage.         

b. Send only representative to seminars and trainings who are directly involved in the implementation of programs and projects and whose jobs are related thereof which can impart the knowledge acquired during the undertaking to minimize expenses considering the limited resources of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Personnel concerned were sent the copy of the ND for them to refund the amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management refrain from allowing any employee to undertake the collection activities of the local government unit if he/she is not a regular appointee and officially designated to perform collection function. Moreover, require all accountable officers to apply for the proper amount of fidelity bond sufficient to cover the handled cash accountabilities so as to safeguard government funds and protect the interest of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the issuances of Official Receipts to casual employees

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the management:

Require the municipal treasurer, at the beginning of the year, to prepare the certified list of taxpayer with the amount due and collectible for the year and submit to the municipal accountant for recording.
Require the municipal accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the debit to Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable and the credit to Deferred Real Property Tax Income/Deferred Special Education Tax Income based on the list submitted by the municipal treasurer.
Require the municipal treasurer to prepare a current list of delinquent taxpayers with the amount due and collectible and submit to the municipal accountant for reconciliation of records to establish a correct balance of RPT/SET Receivables.
Require the municipal accountant to make the necessary adjustment in the books based on the correct RPT/SET balance.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the certified list  of taxpayer with the amount due and collectible for the year and submit to the municipal accountant for recording.
Instructed the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort to augment income by collecting taxes of the  delinquent taxpayers

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:


      We recommend the management to take action on  our  recommendation  by
requiring  the Accountable Officer to restitute  the  amount  due  from  him
and/or institute administrative sanctions  to  recover  the  misappropriated
funds.

Recommendation:

            We recommend management to:

      a. Require the OIC- Municipal  Treasurer  and  the  Property  Disposal
         Committee the application for  disposal  of  properties  which  are
         unserviceable and no longer needed by the LGU  in  compliance  with
         above- cited provision.

      b. Drop from the books the value  of  the  disposed  properties  after
         adjustments and approval.

Recommendation:


        Require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to
monitor and control disbursements of the fund by not exceeding  fifty  (50%)
of the uncollected estimated revenue in addition to the actual  collections,
provided, however, that no  cash  overdraft  in  any  local  fund  shall  be
incurred at the end of the year.

Recommendation:

       Require the Municipal Treasurer to fully release the shares  intended
to the Provincial Government and 35 barangays concerned pursuant to  Section
271 of RA 7160 in order for these  LGUs  to  utilize  said  fund  for  their
operations.

Recommendation:

                        We recommend that management:

      1. Require the officials/employees concerned to immediately  liquidate
         their outstanding cash  advances.  Withhold  any  claim/payment  of
         these personnel concerned until such time their cash  advances  are
         fully liquidated.

      2. Require  strict compliance  with the  provisions  of  COA  Circular
         No. 97-002 and 96-004  in the grant, utilization and liquidation of
          cash advances. Do not grant additional  cash  advance  unless  the
         previous advance is first liquidated.

Recommendation:

              We recommend that the Municipal Accountant exert extra  effort
to review  the  long  outstanding/stale  checks  specifically  those  checks
without the identified payee. Require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel  the
unclaimed  stale  checks  and  furnish  the  accountant  with  the  original
Disbursement  Vouchers  (DV)  and  its   supporting   documents,   for   the
preparation  of  the  Journal  Entry  Voucher   following   the   procedures
prescribed to ensure recording of such cancellation.

Recommendation:

             We recommend that Management maximize the  utilization  of  70%
of the LDRRMF appropriation to support disaster risk  management  activities
and develop the resiliency of constituents in times of calamities.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that Management advice the Municipal Accountant  to  make
the  necessary  adjustments  in  the  books   to  transfer  the   unexpended
appropriation of the CY 2019 LDRRMF to a special trust fund under the  Trust
Fund intended solely for the purpose of supporting disaster  risk  reduction
and management activities within the next five (5) years.

Recommendation:

       We recommend that management  require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer  to
safeguard the accountable forms by keeping it  in  a  secure  place  at  all
times preferably in a locked fireproof or safe in a locked  office  strictly
accessible to authorized personnel only to preclude the accountable forms
from possible misuse  and  loss  due  to  theft.  Further,  the  logbook  of
accountable forms has to be monitored frequently.


      We also recommend the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to account  the  missing
AFs as well as the collections from its issuances.

Require the management officials to appoint the mandatory position of a qualified Municipal Engineer to ensure prompt implementation of development projects and activities and coordinate in its full implementation as programmed by the LGU in order to achieve the intended purpose for which the fund was appropriated and improve the socio-economic and environmental condition of the constituents in compliance with Joint Memorandum Circular No, 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommend that management appoint/designate a qualified official/employee to act as Liquidating Officer in order to safeguard government resources to ensure a sound internal control system in compliance with Section 50, Vol. III of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual.
observations.managementAction: Acted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management exercise due diligence in the utilization and safekeeping of government properties. Moreover, require justification on the discrepancies in the quantity per purchase order against the actual physical count.
observations.managementAction: Acted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management totally relieve the Municipal Treasurer from his position and henceforth, preclude him from any designation/function that involves handling of cash. Further, require him to fully restitute the missing funds of ₱ 604,351.07.
observations.managementAction: Sent demand letters to the concerned accountable officer

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management refrain from allowing any employee to undertake the collection activities of the local government unit if he/she is not a regular appointee and officially designated to perform collection function. Moreover, require all accountable officers to apply for the proper amount of fidelity bond sufficient to cover the handled cash accountabilities so as to safeguard government funds and protect the interest of the municipality.

We recommend the Municipal Treasurer to remit all taxes withheld by the municipality to the Bureau of Internal Revenue on the 10th day of the month following the month in which the withholding was made to avoid payment of surcharges and penalties that could be charged against his private funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the implementors/initiators of the municipality who are involved in the activities regarding project undertaking to implement the projects within the year to improve the socio-economic condition of the whole constituents. Further, require them to justify and explain for the delay on said program/projects.

Submit immediately the required supporting documents to prove the validity and accuracy of the concerned claims to avoid disallowance in audit.

observations.recommendation:
a). Management require all department heads to submit their Annual Procurement Plan to the BAC Secretariat who will prepare the LGUs Annual Procurement Plan based on the consolidated listings of supplies allocation for the year.  All procurement during the year should be in accordance with the approved Annual Procurement Plan.

The Municipal Officials concerned should stop the practice of acquiring goods on a staggered basis thru shopping and adhere to the provisions of Section 10 of Article IV of Republic Act 9184, which requires that all procurement shall be done through competitive public bidding through the Bidding and Award Committee (BAC) to ensure that procurement of goods and services are obtained at process which are most advantageous to the government.  Limit the use of alternative mode of procurement only when circumstances warrant and fall within the provisions of Section 52 of Rule XVI of RA 9184.

Require concerned local officials to observe austerity measures in the disbursement of funds and refrain from incurring unnecessary expenses to spare government funds from wastage.

Send only representative to seminars and trainings who are directly involved in the implementation of programs and projects and whose jobs are related thereof which can impart the knowledge acquired during the undertaking to minimize expenses considering the limited resources of the LGU.

Require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to monitor and control disbursements of the fund by not exceeding fifty (50%) of the uncollected estimated revenue in addition to the actual collections, provided, however, that no cash overdraft in any local fund shall be incurred at the end of the year.

We recommend that Management maximize the utilization of 70% of the LDRRMF appropriation to support disaster risk management activities and develop the resiliency of constituents in times of calamities.

observations.recommendation:
Consolidate the requisition for one or more items needed at or about the same time by the requisitioner.

Filing of the requisitions shall be done in advance to allow procurement through public bidding.

Prepare only one  Annual Procurement Plan. 
   
Stop the practice of acquiring goods and services on a staggered basis thru petty cash fund.

Comply strictly with the provisions of Section 7.1 and 10 of the Revised RA 9184 on the procurement of medicines which should be in accordance with an approved Annual Procurement Plan to ensure maximum and efficient utilization of resources by preventing or minimizing the possibility of excessive and wasteful procurement.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the GSO to review all of the Memorandum Receipts (MRs) paying special attention to those issued many years ago and facilitate the revision of all obsolete ones and replace them with the current form prescribed under the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Vol. I which is the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE).
b. Direct  the  GSO   and  all  persons concerned to cause the renewal of all AREs once every three (3) years or whenever there is a change in the custodianship of municipal properties.
c. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to go over all of the properties of the LGU and see to it that each one is appropriately covered by an ARE.
Remind all officers and employees who have been entrusted with the use of government properties to exercise due care in the utilization and safekeeping thereof as they are the ones primarily responsible in case of loss or damage to the properties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Management direct  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
record all the transactions pertaining to collections and  deposits  of  the
General Fund daily or regularly pursuant  to  Sections  26  and  27  of  the
Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs  and  in  consonance
with sound internal control in order  to  keep  his  official  cash  records
intact and thus preclude the loss or pilferage thereof.

We  recommend  that  the  municipal  officials  have  to  properly
program/plan the implementation of the  project  and  comply  the  necessary
requirements to assure its  completion.  Moreover,  they  should  thoroughly
evaluate the viability of the project  before  procurement  is  effected  to
ensure the attainment of intended benefits  and to avoid wastage of funds.

We recommend that management:

   1. Require the municipal treasurer,  at the beginning of  the  year,   to
      prepare the certified  list  of  taxpayer  with  the  amount  due  and
      collectible for the year and submit to the  municipal  accountant  for
      recording.

   2. Require the municipal accountant to   draw  a  Journal  Entry  Voucher
      (JEV) to record the debit  to  Real  Property  Tax  Receivable/Special
      Education Tax Receivable and the credit to Deferred Real Property  Tax
      Income/Deferred  Special  Education  Tax  Income  based  on  the  list
      submitted by the municipal treasurer.


   3.  Require  the  municipal  treasurer  to  prepare  a  current  list  of
      delinquent taxpayers  with the amount due and collectible  and  submit
      to the municipal accountant for reconciliation of records to establish
      a correct balance of RPT/SET Receivables.


   4. Require the municipal accountant to make the necessary  adjustment  in
      the books based on the correct RPT/SET balance.

We recommend that the management:





    a. Require concerned local officials to observe austerity  measures  in
       the disbursement of funds and  refrain  from  incurring  unnecessary
       expenses to spare government funds from wastage.



    b. Send only representative to seminars and trainings who are  directly
       involved in the implementation of programs and  projects  and  whose
       jobs are related thereof which can  impart  the  knowledge  acquired
       during the undertaking to minimize expenses considering the  limited
       resources of the LGU.

Require the Municipal Treasurer to release the shares intended to  the
local government units concerned pursuant to  Section  286  of  RA  7160  in
order to utilize said fund for their operations.

We  recommend  that  management  require  the  implementors/initiators
of  the  municipality  who  are  involved  in   the  activities  regarding
project undertaking to implement  the  projects  within  the  year   to
improve the socio-economic condition of the whole constituents. Further, require them to justify and explain for the delay  on
said program/projects.

We recommend the Municipal  Treasurer to remit all taxes withheld   by
the municipality  to the Bureau of Internal Revenue on the 10th day  of  the
month following the month  in  which  the  withholding  was  made  to  avoid
payment of surcharges and  penalties  that  could  be  charged  against  his
private funds.

We recommend that management appropriate separately 1% of its IRA  for
LCPC related programs, activities and projects pursuant to Section 15 of  RA
9344.  Further,  adhere  strictly  to  the  guidelines  set  forth  in  DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommend that:


      a. The Municipal Accountant have to record  unserviceable  assets  to
         the  “Other  Assets”  account  for  proper  presentation  in   the
         financial statements;



      b. The Municipal Accountant make the  necessary  adjustments  in  the
         books based on the result of the actual count  and  reconciliation
         activities;


      c. Require  the  Municipal  Treasurer  and  Municipal  Accountant  to
         maintain stock/ property cards and ledger cards  respectively,  to
         properly account the receipt and disposition of the properties and
         supplies.

Recommendation:

           We recommend that management:

     1. Require  the municipal  treasurer  to  impose  the  remittance  of
        collections intact  daily  from  the revenue  collectors   to  the
        Liquidating Officer. Likewise, the Liquidating Officer shall   turn
        over  all collections received by her  to the Municipal Treasurer.


     2.  Require  the  municipal  treasurer  to  deposit  intact  all   his
        collections to the authorized depository bank daily  or  not  later
        than the next banking day.

Recommendation:


          Require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal  Accountant
to monitor and control disbursements of the  fund  by  not  exceeding  fifty
(50%) of the  uncollected  estimated  revenue  in  addition  to  the  actual
collections, provided, however, that no cash overdraft  in  any  local  fund
shall be incurred at the end of the year.

Recommendation:
            We recommend that management:

      a) See to it that PCSO Endowment Funds be fully utilized to help
         address the health and medical needs  and benefit the indigent
         constituents of the municipality.



      b) Assign a competent personnel to handle and perform the  processing
         and facilitating  the  documentary  requirements  to  support  the
         claim.


      c) Submit the required liquidation report to the PCSO to  avail   the
         succeeding release of the fund that may be claimed by the LGU.

Recommendation:


     1. Require the Municipal Planning and Development Council to  maximize
        the utilization of  the  20%  Development  Fund  so  that  all  its
        programmed development projects could be implemented immediately to
        ensure that intended beneficiaries avail of  the  maximum  benefits
        that will be derived therefrom.


     2. Strictly observe the provisions of Section 287 of Republic Act 7160
        and  Joint Circular 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG and see to  it  that
        the  20%  Development  Fund  is  used  for  programs  and  projects
        specifically outlined therein.

Recommendation:

       1.  Require  the  Chairperson  of  the  GAD  Focal  Point  System  to
          coordinate and conduct meeting with the different LGU offices  and
          national government agencies involved in the implementation of the
          planned GAD programs and activities.


       2. Require the concerned officials involved to provide  justification
          on non-implementation of GAD programs and activities.


       3. Make an  evaluation  as  to  the  clear-cut  causes  of  the  non-
          implementation of these projects and formulate strategies in order
          to  ensure  the  complete  implementation  of  GAD  programs   and
          activities in the future.

Recommendation:

            We recommend that the Municipal  Budget  Officer  to  submit  an
explanation for allowing those obligations not related to LDRRMF be  charged
against the fund. Further, utilize the special fund only  for  its  intended
purposes as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002

Recommendation:

        Require the Municipal Accountant to investigate/trace  the  negative
balances of the Cash in Vault account  in  the  General  Fund  and  Special
Education Fund and effect in  the  agency  books  the  necessary  adjusting
entries in order to present the correct amount of Cash in Vault account  in
the financial statements.

Recommendation:

      Appoint/designate a qualified official/employee to act as  Liquidating
Officer in order  to  safeguard  government  resources  to  ensure  a  sound
internal control system in compliance with  Section  50,  Vol.  III  of  the
Government Accounting and Auditing Manual.
